page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-2 is an entry-level, compute-focused virtual machine instance within the E2 family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon core with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers of cloud compute resources. Single-core stress-ng performance registers at 1105.98, while multi-core performance reaches 1452.89, both falling within the bottom quartile of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with uncached read speeds at 13.03 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.11 GB/sec. These metrics indicate that while the CPU allocation is dedicated, the overall processing throughput and memory access speeds are limited, making it unsuitable for memory-intensive or high-throughput parallel computing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-highcpu-2 offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of large memory capacities. It is best suited for lightweight, CPU-bound tasks such as basic static web serving, simple text processing, minor compilation tasks, and low-volume Redis operations. It can also handle small-scale language model inference, achieving 164.01 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M parameter model.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-2 is an entry-level, compute-focused virtual machine configured with 2 vCPUs and 2.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, this instance features a dedicated CPU allocation of 1 physical core with 2 threads. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers, with a Geekbench single-core score of 906.0 and a multi-core score of 1073.0. Memory bandwidth is also modest, peaking at 13.03 GB/sec for uncached reads. Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU, the e2-highcpu-2 is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, compute-bound workloads. It is best suited for basic static web serving, simple text processing, and low-volume database operations where memory demands are minimal.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-2 is a budget-friendly virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for lightweight, compute-bound workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-focused virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and a highly streamlined memory profile for lightweight workloads.

Equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory (1.0 GB per vCPU)
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for lightweight static web serving and simple text processing
Cost-efficient option for low-memory, CPU-bound tasks
